sound_type: high bleat -- high-pitched, nasal, wavering contact call given by pups to locate mothers. Distinct from adult guttural bark. E=4
ecosystem: Sandy beaches, tidal flats, protected coves. Pupping season: late spring to early summer. Pup stays on beach while mother forages.
season: Pupping: May-August, peak June-July. Nursing period: 4-6 weeks. Weaning abrupt.

coordinates.description: Harbor Seal pup on Puget Sound / San Juan Islands beaches. Summer pupping season.

taxonomy_note.description: Second Harbor Seal entry. Fifth species with double entries. Second Phocidae. Adult (180) = guttural bark. Pup (181) = high bleat. Different sound, same species.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Phocidae: Harbor Seal adult (180), Harbor Seal pup (181) -- 2 entries.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Pup = young seal. The high bleat is the defining sound of the Harbor Seal pup on PNW beaches.
taxonomy_note.double_entry_note: Fifth species with double entries. Each entry captures a distinct acoustic dimension: adult bark vs pup bleat.

song_metrics.song_duration_s: Individual bleat: 0.3-1.0 seconds. Series: 3-20+ calls with brief pauses.
song_metrics.note_structure: High, nasal, wavering bleat. Lamb-like quality. Urgent. Repeated.
song_metrics.note_types: Contact bleat (primary), distress call, recognition exchange with mother.
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Bleat series: 3-20+ calls, brief pauses between. Persistent when separated.
song_metrics.timbre: Nasal, high-pitched, wavering. Distinct from adult guttural bark.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=4. Moderate per call but persistent repetition. Carries across beach.
song_metrics.tempo: Bleat rate: ~1-2 per second during active calling. Variable with distress level.
song_metrics.context: Beach pupping sites. Separation, contact, distress, nursing reunion.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Minimal. Individual vocal signatures for mother-pup recognition.
song_metrics.key_property: HIGH BLEAT. Mother-pup vocal recognition. Second Harbor Seal entry. Fifth double-entry species. E=4. v1.49.322.

pelage.overall: Short gray to brown fur, spotted/ringed pattern. Born with adult-type coat (lanugo shed in utero). Lighter than adults.
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Smaller size. Lighter coloring. Round face with proportionally larger eyes. On beach alone during pupping season.
pelage.seasonal_variation: Pup coat is the birth coat. First molt after weaning.
pelage.size_at_birth: Length: ~75-100 cm. Weight: ~8-12 kg. Gain ~0.5 kg/day during nursing.

nodes.title: Mother-Pup Vocal Recognition
nodes.description: Individual vocal signatures recognized within hours of birth. Frequency contour, timing, and harmonic structure encode identity. Acoustic bond critical in crowded colonies.

nodes.title: High Bleat vs Guttural Bark
nodes.description: Same species, same energy, different sound. Adult: low bark (200-2000 Hz, alarm). Pup: high bleat (500-3000 Hz, contact). Ontogenetic vocal shift.

nodes.title: Fifth Double Entry Species
nodes.description: Harbor Seal becomes the fifth species with two entries in the engine. Each captures a distinct acoustic and ecological dimension.

cross_references.relationship: DOUBLE ENTRY! Adult bark (180) vs pup bleat (181). Same species, same energy, different sound and function. The pair within the species.
PINNIPED EXPANSION! From Phocidae (true seals, 180-181) to Otariidae (eared seals, 182). Marine mammal era continues.
PRECOCIAL CONTRAST! Murrelet chick departs nest for sea at 2 days. Seal pup stays on beach 4-6 weeks. Different survival strategies.
PAIR BOND! Creative duo chemistry = #1 hits. Pup bleat = mother recognition. Both about the bond that connects two into one.
